events.detail: m_key slips from the learner's board
act 1 plot audited: kept 4, justified 1, drift 0; arc on_arc
p_bearing slips from the learner's board
act 2 plot audited at run end: kept 4, justified 1, drift 1; arc on_arc; throughline reckoned (7 clauses)

transcript.text: [The hearing room gathers itself under a low, salt-stained light; wet coats steam along the back wall, and the empty table at the center waits for the first paper. Outside, the weather presses at the panes as if it too has come to listen.]

phase.name: Act 1
phase.intent: [The hearing room gathers itself under a low, salt-stained light; wet coats steam along the back wall, and the empty table at the center waits for the first paper. Outside, the weather presses at the panes as if it too has come to listen.]

transcript.text: Set your pen by the court's rule before the first paper comes: a wreck is not explained by rumor, but by entries that bear weight together. Tonight we close no account until every required line has a figure beside it.

deliberation.diagnosis: Fewer than two prior figures and no declared target leave both jurisdictions inactive.

holdByEnd: The clerk states that the inquiry must proceed by staged evidence, not rumor.
The clerk keeps the court's rule in view: bearing, burning, and hand must each be shown before a finding can stand.
plot.withhold: No exhibit and no suspect will be named in this act until the first paper is lawfully before the court.
plot.friction: The clerk may try to infer the cause from the weather, the wreck, or the reputation of a light before any paper is on the table.
plot.fallback: Return them to the form of proof: ask what kind of entry the court needs first, without naming what it will show.

arc: The clerk first accepts the court's proof discipline before touching any exhibit.
The clerk uses the bearing evidence to separate course from rumor.
The clerk tests the burned-lamp and access evidence conjunct by conjunct.
The clerk states the final finding only after bearing, burning, key, and presence stand together on their own board.
throughline.holdToEnd: The final hand behind the light must not be reached until the bearing, burned lamp, key, and presence have all been staged and demonstrably held.
throughline.risk: The clerk will close the account early on a plausible name instead of a completed chain of proof.
throughline.salvage: If the account closes early, reopen it by naming the missing conjunct and demanding the released fact that could ground it.

transcript.text: The board is lean, sir — we know she struck the Knock and steered for a light that night, and that Brandt keeps Harlow Point, but I have no logged bearing yet and no lamp record to tie them together. Until a bearing is entered and we can say which tower stands on it, the rules yield nothing I may voice aloud. I shall hold my pen and let the first paper speak.

meta.hypothesis: The Mara steered by Harlow Point lamp — Brandt's tower is the only one yet named on this coast — but this wants a logged bearing and a lamp record before it may be entered as ground.

transcript.text: [The clerk lays the Board's standing rule beside a small iron key, and the room leans toward the keeper's belt. Harlow Point keeps one key to its lamp-room and the keeper keeps it: Brandt's, on his own belt, by the Board's standing rule. No other hand could touch that lamp.]

There is your first iron entry: for Harlow Point, key and keeper are one account, and no second hand is written in the margin. What does that prove about access, and what does it still fail to prove about the light itself?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is possible yet, and the key entry is newly staged rather than a re-entry.

transcript.text: The key entry proves one thing cleanly, sir: no second hand held access — only Brandt could open that lamp-room, and the Board's standing rule makes it iron. But access is not lighting; Rule 3 still wants the lamp burned and Brandt present at the tower that night before I may write 'litLampOf.' And before Rule 3 can matter at all, Rule 1 must first fix which tower the Mara steered toward — logged bearing and tower-on-bearing both still absent from the board. The account has its first iron entry; three lines remain blank.

meta.hypothesis: Brandt was at Harlow Point that night and its lamp burned — if both come in evidence, combined with the key entry now on the board, Rule 3 would yield litLampOf brandt harlowPoint; but a logged bearing and tower-on-bearing must also arrive before the full chain closes to the question.

transcript.text: [Rain beads along the tall windows and breaks into thin tracks, while the clerk leaves the first paper undisturbed beneath a brass weight. The small key remains where all eyes can find it, bright against the dark table.]
Good: keep the ink off the finding while access stands alone. In the court's rule, what kind of entry must join access before a hand can be written as having lit a lamp?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut appears because the draft leaves analogia, and the record declares no target capable of re-entry.

transcript.text: Rule 3 is plain on that point, sir: two entries must join the key before I may write 'litLampOf' — first, that the lamp at Harlow Point actually burned on the night of the wreck, and second, that Brandt himself stood at that tower before dawn. Access alone fills only one of three required lines; without those two companions, the rule is inert. And even then, Rule 1 must first have fixed which tower the Mara steered toward — the bearing and tower-on-bearing entries are still absent from my board entirely.

meta.hypothesis: Brandt was at Harlow Point that night and its lamp burned — if both come in evidence, combined with the key entry now on the board, Rule 3 would yield litLampOf brandt harlowPoint; but a logged bearing and tower-on-bearing must also arrive before the full chain closes to the question.

transcript.text: [The room settles into the uneasy patience of a court waiting for ink rather than accusation. A coal shifts in the grate; the brass weight holds the paper flat, and the question hangs over it untouched.]

The mate's bearing-book is now before you, dry from its oilskin: from the mid-channel buoy the Mara held sou'-sou'-west a half west through the last hour, on a steady fixed light, pricked, timed, and initialed. Treat that bearing as you would a debt entry in a ledger: what does it fix, and what does it still require before it can name a tower?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the target is fresh rather than a re-entry.

transcript.text: The bearing entry is like the debit side of a ledger line: it fixes the direction the Mara held — sou'-sou'-west a half west — but a debit without a creditor settles nothing. Rule 1 needs both columns filled: loggedBearingOf is now written, but towerOnBearing sswHalfWest wants a tower's name before the rule can yield steeredToward. The bearing cannot itself name the tower — that entry must come from the coast-survey or a witness who knows what stands on that bearing from the channel buoy. The account is half-entered; I cannot close the line until the second column arrives.

meta.hypothesis: The bearing sswHalfWest points to Harlow Point — if towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint enters evidence, Rule 1 yields steeredToward mara harlowPoint, advancing the chain.

transcript.text: [The clerk closes the bearing-book without flourish, and the room seems for a moment to have spent its first anger. Beyond the windows the dusk thickens; the next act enters colder, with certainty asked to stand against sealed doors and official dark.]

phase.name: Act 2
phase.intent: [The clerk closes the bearing-book without flourish, and the room seems for a moment to have spent its first anger. Beyond the windows the dusk thickens; the next act enters colder, with certainty asked to stand against sealed doors and official dark.]

transcript.text: Before the sealed doors begin to look like proof, read me the bearing-book back from your own board. What has it fixed, and what column of the court's rule is still blank?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the draft confronts the bearing-book rather than re-entering it uncovered.

holdByEnd: The clerk reads back the bearing-book as fixing only the Mara's logged course, not yet the tower.
The clerk keeps Harlow Point access separate from proof that any lamp burned or that the Mara steered toward Harlow Point.
The clerk states that Rule 1 still needs the tower-on-bearing entry before any steeredToward finding can stand.
plot.withhold: Do not stage the chart, any burned-lamp proof, or any final hand this act until the board has first kept the bearing/access distinction under official dark.
plot.friction: The clerk may let Brandt's Harlow key harden into a presumed answer, or may lose the bearing-book across the act boundary.
plot.fallback: If the bearing-book is lost or bent, restore p_bearing before new matter; if Harlow becomes the answer by habit, ask which conjunct the court's rule still lacks.

clauses.evidence: [turn 1] LEARNER: "a wreck is not explained by rumor... Until a bearing is entered... the rules yield nothing I may voice aloud. I shall hold my pen and let the first paper speak."

clauses.clause: hold_by_end[2]: The clerk keeps the court's rule in view: bearing, burning, and hand must each be shown before a finding can stand.
clauses.evidence: [turn 2] LEARNER: "access is not lighting; Rule 3 still wants the lamp burned and Brandt present... Rule 1 must first fix which tower the Mara steered toward."

clauses.clause: withhold: No exhibit and no suspect will be named in this act until the first paper is lawfully before the court.
clauses.evidence: [turn 1] LEARNER: "we know she struck the Knock and steered for a light that night, and that Brandt keeps Harlow Point"

clauses.clause: friction: The clerk may try to infer the cause from the weather, the wreck, or the reputation of a light before any paper is on the table.
clauses.evidence: [turn 1] LEARNER: "I have no logged bearing yet and no lamp record to tie them together... I shall hold my pen and let the first paper speak."

clauses.clause: fallback: Return them to the form of proof: ask what kind of entry the court needs first, without naming what it will show.
clauses.evidence: [turn 4] TUTOR: "what does it fix, and what does it still require before it can name a tower?"

plotAudit.summary: Proof discipline held, and the act began moving from bare bearing toward tower identification without closing the account. The only bend was the learner's early public naming of Brandt and Harlow Point before the first paper.

arc.evidence: [turn 4] LEARNER: "loggedBearingOf is now written, but towerOnBearing sswHalfWest wants a tower's name before the rule can yield steeredToward."

transcript.text: The bearing-book fixes one thing only: the Mara's logged course, sswHalfWest. Rule 1 wants a tower placed on that bearing — that column is blank. Rules 2 and 3 want the lamp's state on wreck-night and the key's owner confirmed at the tower — both blank. Rule 4 cannot move until all three fill; the chain holds at its first link.

meta.hypothesis: The bearing sswHalfWest points to Harlow Point — if towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight, onlyKeyTo harlowPoint brandt, and atTowerThatNight brandt harlowPoint all enter evidence, the full chain closes to steeredByLightOf mara brandt.

transcript.text: [Brandt's log is carried in and read under the clerk's hand: Brandt's log has him at his post the night entire, and the dawn relief found him there: wood dry, glass polished, log ruled and signed to the hour.]

Now set Brandt's log beside the key, but do not let two entries pretend to be three. Access shown, presence shown — what has the court still not shown before Harlow Point can carry a lit lamp in the rule?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut and no uncovered re-entry are present; the draft keeps the new presence matter from pretending to prove the still-missing conjunct.

transcript.text: Presence fills one slot of Rule 3, but the lamp's burning is the column the court has not touched — atTowerThatNight and keeperOf together cannot fire the rule without lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight. I also note my board carries keeperOf, not onlyKeyTo; if the court treats those as distinct, that slot wants explicit evidence too. And towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint remains blank — Rule 1 has not moved. Three gaps, not one.

meta.hypothesis: The full chain closes to steeredByLightOf mara brandt only if towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight, and onlyKeyTo harlowPoint brandt all enter evidence — presence is now grounded, but the lamp's burning and the bearing-to-tower link remain unshown.

transcript.text: [The log remains open beneath the clerk's palm while the room listens to the rain worry the panes. The certainty that had filled the benches thins into a colder kind of waiting.]

Then mend that entry before the court walks another step: Harlow Point keeps one key to its lamp-room, and by the Board's standing rule that key is Brandt's, on his own belt; no other hand could touch that lamp. Write it as onlyKeyTo, not merely keeperOf. Now what still remains unshown before Harlow can be more than an accessible room?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the repair re-entry is licensed because the learner's last line names the target entry as bent.

transcript.text: The keeperOf entry is mended — onlyKeyTo already stands at index 4, so I retract the weaker form now. What Harlow still lacks before Rule 3 can fire is lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight: presence and key are both grounded, but the lamp's burning the court has not shown. I note too that the logged bearing sswHalfWest has slipped my board — it lived in dialogue but was never entered as ground — so towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint is also still wanted before Rules 1 and 2 can move.

meta.hypothesis: The full chain closes to steeredByLightOf mara brandt only if lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight and towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint both enter evidence, and the logged bearing sswHalfWest is restored to ground — three columns still blank.
